Submit a Source

Sources Library

Cynical Theories

by Helen Pluckrose & James Lindsay

An overview of the evolution of postmodern thought and its application in social justice scholarship.

book

The Madness of Crowds

by Douglas Murray

An examination of the key battlegrounds in the new culture wars: gay rights, feminism, race, and trans rights.

book

What Is Critical Race Theory and Why Is It Under Attack?

by Stephen Sawchuk

An article from Education Week explaining the core tenets of CRT.

article

A Critical Introduction to Critical Race Theory | Erec Smith

by Triggernometry

A YouTube video discussing CRT with Erec Smith.

video

The End of Race Politics: Arguments for a Colorblind America

by Coleman Hughes

A case for colorblind principles in public policy and civic life, with critiques of race-based political frameworks.

book

Social Justice Fallacies

by Thomas Sowell

A critique of common claims made under the banner of social justice, focused on tradeoffs, incentives, and real-world outcomes.

book

Woke Racism: How a New Religion Has Betrayed Black America

by John McWhorter

An argument that modern anti-racism functions like a secular religion, with unintended consequences for public discourse and policy.

book
